summary: Re-verified Landing trending dedup fix. All 4 specced re-check items PASS: (1) DOM first 4 trending rows = [crypto, music, mental-health, confession]; (2) full 8-row topic set = {crypto, music, mental-health, confession, sports, memes, rant, stories}, all unique; (3) confession trending row renders trending-explicit-{id} badge; (4) Music share modal opens with title 'Share this track'. The dedup-by-topic pre-pass in trendList (Landing.jsx lines 152-166) now correctly collapses API duplicates before fallback top-up and slice(0,8).
critical_code_review_comments: Landing.jsx trendList fix is correct: sourceRaw -> dedup-by-topic -> fallback top-up for missing topics -> sort by priority -> slice(0,8). Result is deterministic and verified in DOM.
Suggestion (non-blocking): the IIFE could be wrapped in useMemo([trending]) to avoid recomputation on every render, though the cost is negligible for n=8.
test_credentials: N/A (anonymous flow; no auth required for this verification)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Landing trending fix is verified. Observed live DOM order: [crypto, music, mental-health, confession, sports, memes, rant, stories]. Music share modal opens with correct title. No further action needed for this iteration's spec.
rca of the issue: Previously, when API trending returned multiple posts of the same topic (e.g., 2x music from music-chatter bot), the seenTopics set treated duplicates as 'seen', so the FALLBACK top-up skipped them and slice(0,8) dropped the lowest-priority real topic ('stories'). Fix: pre-dedup the API list to keep only the first occurrence per topic, then top-up from FALLBACK_POSTS for missing topics. Verified in DOM after fix.
